summary:
The lateral shift affects facial-lingual and mesio-distal tooth movements due to changes in biting forces; these modifications influence bite pattern and dental alignment significantly.
The lateral shift impacts tooth position along two main axes through altered occlusal contacts, affecting how upper and lower teeth meet when moving side-to-side or front-back. To summarize concisely while focusing only on critical aspects directly pertinent to understanding the effect of a lateral shift as per your request involves highlighting primary outcomes such as positional adjustments within specific planes influenced by mechanical interactions between opposing dentitions under different jaw motions. This summary encapsulates essential information regarding what happens clinically at macroscopic levels concerning individual tooth displacements resulting from lateral shifts without delving into less crucial details about underlying mechanisms not explicitly asked about here.
